questions.stem: Which formula is used to calculate Cost Performance Index?
questions.explanation: CPI = EV divided by AC. It measures cost efficiency. If CPI is above 1 the project is under budget.

questions.stem: Which formula is used to calculate Schedule Performance Index?
questions.explanation: SPI equals EV divided by PV. It measures schedule efficiency.

questions.stem: A project has EV = 400 and AC = 500. What is the Cost Variance?
questions.explanation: CV = EV minus AC. 400 minus 500 equals negative 100 meaning the project is over budget.

questions.stem: A project has EV = 300 and PV = 400. What is the Schedule Variance?
questions.explanation: SV = EV minus PV. 300 minus 400 equals negative 100 indicating the project is behind schedule.

questions.stem: A project has EV = 600 and AC = 500. What is CPI?
questions.explanation: CPI = EV divided by AC. 600 divided by 500 equals 1.2 which means the project is under budget.

questions.stem: A project has EV = 450 and PV = 600. What is SPI?
questions.explanation: SPI = EV divided by PV. 450 divided by 600 equals 0.75 meaning the project is behind schedule.

questions.stem: Which formula estimates the future total project cost using CPI?
questions.explanation: EAC equals BAC divided by CPI when current cost performance is expected to continue.

questions.stem: Which formula calculates Estimate to Complete?
questions.explanation: ETC equals EAC minus AC and represents how much more money is required to finish the project.
